---
title: "HERMES_24_Analysis_Clean"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-20"
---

```{r}
# REQUIRED PACKAGES
# -- R version 4.4.2

library(pROC)
library(gtsummary)
library(tidyverse)
library(gtsummary)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggpubr)
library(boot)
library(caret)
library(rms)
library(lubridate)
library(systemfonts)
library(rlang)
library(binom)
```

```{r}
            # See data dictionary for dataset variables and definition
```


```{r}
# 1 -- Discriminative Performance and Calibration Metrics for Binary Outcomes --
# HELPER FUNCTION 1

evaluate_binary_outcome <- function(data, outcome_var, age_var, nihss_var,
                                    title_suffix = "",
                                    negate_score = TRUE) {
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Compute raw and centred HERMES-24 score
  # ----------------------------------------
  raw_score  <- 0.1 * data[[age_var]] + 1 * data[[nihss_var]]
  score_mean <- mean(raw_score, na.rm = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("\nMean HERMES-24 score in cohort: %.4f\n", score_mean))
  
  # Negate for good outcomes (mRS <=2, mRS <=3)
  # Do not negate for mortality
  if (negate_score) {
    data$fixed_score           <- -(raw_score - score_mean)  # centred
    data$fixed_score_uncentred <- -(raw_score)               # uncentred
    score_direction_label      <- "- 0.1000 * (age - mean_age/10) - 1.0000 * (NIHSS - mean_NIHSS)"
  } else {
    data$fixed_score           <-  (raw_score - score_mean)  # centred
    data$fixed_score_uncentred <-  (raw_score)               # uncentred
    score_direction_label      <- "+ 0.1000 * (age - mean_age/10) + 1.0000 * (NIHSS - mean_NIHSS)"
  }
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Model 1a — centred intercept-only model
  # ----------------------------------------
  logistic_model <- glm(
    as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ offset(fixed_score)")),
    data   = data,
    family = binomial(link = "logit")
  )
  
  intercept    <- coef(logistic_model)[1]
  intercept_se <- summary(logistic_model)$coefficients[1, 2]
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Model 1b — uncentred intercept-only model
  # ----------------------------------------
  logistic_model_uncentred <- glm(
    as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ offset(fixed_score_uncentred)")),
    data   = data,
    family = binomial(link = "logit")
  )
  
  intercept_uncentred    <- coef(logistic_model_uncentred)[1]
  intercept_uncentred_se <- summary(logistic_model_uncentred)$coefficients[1, 2]
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Print prediction equations
  # ----------------------------------------
  cat("\n--- Full Prediction Equation:", title_suffix, "---\n")
  cat(sprintf("Mean HERMES-24 score (centring value): %.4f\n", score_mean))
  cat(sprintf("Log-odds equation (centred):\n"))
  cat(sprintf("  log(p / 1-p) = %.4f %s\n", intercept, score_direction_label))
  cat(sprintf("Predicted probability equation:\n"))
  cat(sprintf("  p = 1 / (1 + exp(-(%.4f %s)))\n", intercept, score_direction_label))
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Print intercept comparison
  # ----------------------------------------
  cat("\n--- Intercept Comparison:", title_suffix, "---\n")
  cat(sprintf("Uncentred intercept: %.4f (95%% CI: %.4f to %.4f)\n",
              intercept_uncentred,
              intercept_uncentred - 1.96 * intercept_uncentred_se,
              intercept_uncentred + 1.96 * intercept_uncentred_se))
  cat(sprintf("  Refers to: log-odds at HERMES-24 = 0 (age 0, NIHSS 0 — impossible patient)\n"))
  cat(sprintf("Centred intercept:   %.4f (95%% CI: %.4f to %.4f)\n",
              intercept,
              intercept - 1.96 * intercept_se,
              intercept + 1.96 * intercept_se))
  cat(sprintf("  Refers to: log-odds at mean HERMES-24 score = %.2f (typical patient in cohort)\n", score_mean))
  cat(sprintf("  Equivalent probability at mean score: %.4f\n", plogis(intercept)))
  cat(sprintf("  Observed outcome rate:                %.4f\n", mean(data[[outcome_var]], na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("  Difference (calibration-in-the-large): %.4f\n",
              plogis(intercept) - mean(data[[outcome_var]], na.rm = TRUE)))
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Calibration-in-the-large (centred)
  # ----------------------------------------
  cat("\n--- Calibration-in-the-large ---\n")
  cat(sprintf("Centred intercept: %.4f (95%% CI: %.4f to %.4f)\n",
              intercept,
              intercept - 1.96 * intercept_se,
              intercept + 1.96 * intercept_se))
  cat(sprintf("Equivalent probability: %.4f\n", plogis(intercept)))
  cat(sprintf("Observed outcome rate:  %.4f\n", mean(data[[outcome_var]], na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat("(Intercept of 0 = perfect calibration-in-the-large)\n")
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Predict probabilities from centred model
  # ----------------------------------------
  predicted_probs <- predict(logistic_model, type = "response")
  
  # Diagnostics
  cat(sprintf("\nPredicted prob range: %.4f to %.4f\n",
              min(predicted_probs), max(predicted_probs)))
  cat(sprintf("Mean predicted probability: %.4f\n", mean(predicted_probs)))
  cat(sprintf("Observed outcome rate:      %.4f\n", mean(data[[outcome_var]], na.rm = TRUE)))
  cat(sprintf("Correlation with outcome:   %.4f\n",
              cor(predicted_probs, data[[outcome_var]], use = "complete.obs")))
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Calibration slope model (centred score, both parameters free)
  # ----------------------------------------
  cal_slope_model <- glm(
    as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ fixed_score")),
    data   = data,
    family = binomial(link = "logit")
  )
  
  cal_intercept    <- coef(cal_slope_model)[1]
  cal_intercept_se <- summary(cal_slope_model)$coefficients[1, 2]
  cal_slope        <- coef(cal_slope_model)[2]
  cal_slope_se     <- summary(cal_slope_model)$coefficients[2, 2]
  
  cat("\n--- Calibration slope model ---\n")
  cat(sprintf("Intercept: %.4f (95%% CI: %.4f to %.4f)\n",
              cal_intercept,
              cal_intercept - 1.96 * cal_intercept_se,
              cal_intercept + 1.96 * cal_intercept_se))
  cat(sprintf("Slope:     %.4f (95%% CI: %.4f to %.4f)\n",
              cal_slope,
              cal_slope - 1.96 * cal_slope_se,
              cal_slope + 1.96 * cal_slope_se))
  cat("(Slope of 1.0 = perfect calibration across range of predictions)\n")
  cat("(Slope < 1.0 = predictions too extreme; slope > 1.0 = too conservative)\n")
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # ROC curve
  # ----------------------------------------
  roc_obj <- roc(response    = data[[outcome_var]],
                 predictor   = predicted_probs,
                 direction   = "<",
                 legacy.axes = TRUE,
                 plot        = TRUE,
                 col         = "#377eb8",
                 lwd         = 1.5,
                 print.auc   = TRUE,
                 print.auc.x = 0.7,
                 print.auc.y = 1.0,
                 ci          = TRUE,
                 main        = paste("ROC Curve", title_suffix))
  
  auc_ci <- ci.auc(roc_obj, method = "bootstrap", boot.n = 1000)
  cat(sprintf("\nAUC: %.3f [%.3f, %.3f]\n", roc_obj$auc, auc_ci[1], auc_ci[3]))
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Brier score
  # ----------------------------------------
  true_outcomes <- data[[outcome_var]]
  brier_score   <- mean((predicted_probs - true_outcomes)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  cat(sprintf("Brier Score: %.4f\n", brier_score))
  
# ----------------------------------------
# Calibration plot (decile binning + shaded 95% CI ribbon)
# ----------------------------------------

data$predicted_prob <- predicted_probs
data$decile         <- ntile(data$predicted_prob, 10)

# Create calibration summary table
calibration_data <- data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(decile) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_predicted      = mean(predicted_prob, na.rm = TRUE),
    observed_proportion = mean(.data[[outcome_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
    n                   = dplyr::n(),
    events              = sum(.data[[outcome_var]], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups             = "drop"
  )

# Add exact binomial 95% confidence intervals
ci_data <- binom.confint(
  x      = calibration_data$events,
  n      = calibration_data$n,
  method = "exact"
)

calibration_data <- calibration_data %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    lower_ci = ci_data$lower,
    upper_ci = ci_data$upper
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(mean_predicted)

# Generate calibration plot
cal_plot <- ggplot(
  calibration_data,
  aes(x = mean_predicted,
      y = observed_proportion)
) +

  # Shaded confidence interval ribbon
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = lower_ci,
        ymax = upper_ci),
    fill  = "blue",
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +

  # Calibration line
  geom_line(
    color = "blue",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +

  # Calibration points
  geom_point(
    color = "blue",
    size  = 2
  ) +

  # Perfect calibration reference line
  geom_abline(
    slope     = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype  = "dashed",
    color     = "black"
  ) +

  labs(
    title = paste("Calibration Plot", title_suffix),

    subtitle = paste0(
      "Brier Score: ", round(brier_score, 4),
      " | Slope: ", round(cal_slope, 3),
      " | Intercept (centred): ", round(intercept, 3),
      " | Intercept (uncentred): ", round(intercept_uncentred, 3)
    ),

    x = "Mean predicted probability (decile)",
    y = "Observed proportion"
  ) +

  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)
  ) +

  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)
  ) +

  theme_pubr() +

  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face  = "bold"
    ),

    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      size  = 8
    )
  )

#return(cal_plot)

# Used for yearly analysis
return(list(
  plot          = cal_plot,
  auc           = as.numeric(roc_obj$auc),
  ci_lower      = auc_ci[1],
  ci_upper      = auc_ci[3],
  brier         = brier_score,
  slope         = cal_slope,
  intercept     = intercept,
  intercept_unc = intercept_uncentred,
  n             = nrow(data)
))

}
```

```{r}
# 1 -- Discriminative Performance and Calibration Metrics for Binary Outcomes --
# Example Usage 

    # Set negate_score = TRUE for positive outcomes (mRS 2 & 3)
    #     negate_score = FALSE for negative outcomes (90d mortality)

# -- WHOLE COHORT MRS2 -- 
# Subset your data to preclude patients with baseline mRS >2 

Hermes_2_FINAL <- Hermes_FINAL_vessel[Hermes_FINAL_vessel$estimated_prestroke_mrs <= 2,]

plot_mrs2 <- evaluate_binary_outcome(data = Hermes_2_FINAL,
                        outcome_var = "mRS_2",
                        age_var = "age_at_stroke",
                        nihss_var = "nihss_24_hrs_prior_reperfusion_therapy",
                        title_suffix = "for mRS ≤ 2",
                        negate_score = TRUE)

# -- WHOLE COHORT MRS 3 --
# Subset your data to preclude patients with baseline mRS >3
Hermes_3_FINAL <- Hermes_FINAL_vessel[Hermes_FINAL_vessel$estimated_prestroke_mrs <= 3,]

plot_mrs3 <- evaluate_binary_outcome(data = Hermes_3_FINAL,
                        outcome_var = "mRS_3",
                        age_var = "age_at_stroke",
                        nihss_var = "nihss_24_hrs_prior_reperfusion_therapy",
                        title_suffix = "for mRS ≤ 3", 
                        negate_score = TRUE)

# -- WHOLE COHORT MORTALITY -- 
# No sub-setting is required as all patients even with pre-existing disability can be included for this analysis. 

plot_mortality <- evaluate_binary_outcome(data = Hermes_FINAL_vessel,
                        outcome_var = "timedif_mortality_lastknown_90d",
                        age_var = "age_at_stroke",
                        nihss_var = "nihss_24_hrs_prior_reperfusion_therapy",
                        title_suffix = "Mortality at 90d",
                        negate_score = FALSE)

```

```{r}
# 2 -- Ordinal Analysis --

# CREATE DATASET FOR ORDINAL ANALYSIS
# By convention (mRS 5 and 6 are treated as the same class)
Hermes_ordinal <- Hermes_FINAL_vessel
Hermes_ordinal$x3_month_mrs[Hermes_FINAL_vessel$x3_month_mrs == 6] <-5
```

```{r}
# 2 -- Ordinal Analysis --
# HELPER FUNCTION 2 - Ordinal Concordance Index 

concordance_index <- function(outcome, predicted_score) {
  n <- length(outcome)
  concordant <- 0
  discordant <- 0
  
  for (i in 1:(n - 1)) {
    for (j in (i + 1):n) {
      if (outcome[i] != outcome[j]) {
        if ((outcome[i] > outcome[j] && predicted_score[i] > predicted_score[j]) ||
            (outcome[i] < outcome[j] && predicted_score[i] < predicted_score[j])) {
          concordant <- concordant + 1
        } else if ((outcome[i] > outcome[j] && predicted_score[i] < predicted_score[j]) ||
                   (outcome[i] < outcome[j] && predicted_score[i] > predicted_score[j])) {
          discordant <- discordant + 1
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  concordance_index <- concordant / (concordant + discordant)
  return(concordance_index)
}

#Bootstrap function
bootstrap_c_index <- function(data, n_bootstraps = 1000) {
  boot_results <- numeric(n_bootstraps)
  
  for (i in 1:n_bootstraps) {
    boot_sample <- data[sample(1:nrow(data), replace = TRUE), ]
    boot_results[i] <- concordance_index(boot_sample$outcome, boot_sample$predicted)
  }
  
  return(boot_results)
}

#Evaluate ordinal outcome for single dataset
evaluate_ordinal_outcome <- function(data, outcome_var, predictor_var,
                                     title_suffix = "", n_bootstraps = 100) {
  outcome <- data[[outcome_var]]
  predicted <- data[[predictor_var]]
  
  # Original concordance index
  c_index_val <- concordance_index(outcome, predicted)
  
  # Bootstrap CI
  boot_data <- data.frame(outcome = outcome, predicted = predicted)
  boot_results <- bootstrap_c_index(boot_data, n_bootstraps)
  ci_lower <- quantile(boot_results, 0.025)
  ci_upper <- quantile(boot_results, 0.975)
  
  # Output
  cat("Ordinal Concordance Index", if (!is.null(title_suffix)) paste0(" ", title_suffix), 
      ":", round(c_index_val, 3), "\n")
  cat("95% CI:", round(ci_lower, 3), "–", round(ci_upper, 3), "\n")
  
  return(list(c_index = c_index_val, ci_lower = ci_lower, ci_upper = ci_upper))
}

```

```{r}
# 2 -- Ordinal Analysis --
# Example, ordinal discriminative perform for 3 month mRS

evaluate_ordinal_outcome(
  data = Hermes_ordinal,
  outcome_var = "x3_month_mrs",
  predictor_var = "HERMES_Score",
  title_suffix = "Discriminative performance for ordinal mRS", 
  n_bootstraps = 100
)

```


```{r}
# 3 -- SUBGROUP ANALYSIS OF BINARY OUTCOMES --
# Builds upon "evaluate_binary_outcome" function defined previously for binary outcome analysis, with wrapper function 

evaluate_subgroups <- function(data, subgroup_var, subgroup_labels, subgroup_conditions,
                               outcome_var, age_var, nihss_var, outcome_label = NULL) {
  
  stopifnot(length(subgroup_labels) == length(subgroup_conditions))
  
  for (i in seq_along(subgroup_labels)) {
    label <- subgroup_labels[i]
    condition_expr <- subgroup_conditions[[i]]
    
    # Subset data
    subgroup_data <- data %>% filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(condition_expr))
    n <- nrow(subgroup_data)
    
    cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
    cat("Subgroup:", label, "\n")
    cat("N =", n, "\n")
    
    if (n < 30) {
      warning(paste("Subgroup", label, "has < 30 patients. Results may be unstable."))
    }
    
    evaluate_binary_outcome(subgroup_data, outcome_var, age_var, nihss_var,
                            title_suffix = paste0("(", label, ") ", outcome_label))
  }
}


```

```{r}
# 3 -- SUBGROUP ANALYSIS OF BINARY OUTCOMES --
# EXAMPLE USAGE (stratifying by patients aged >70 and <= 70) for binary outcome mRS <= 2

evaluate_subgroups(
  data = Hermes_2_FINAL,
  subgroup_var = "age_at_stroke",
  subgroup_labels = c("Age < 70", "Age ≥ 70"),
  subgroup_conditions = c("age_at_stroke < 70", "age_at_stroke >= 70"),
  outcome_var = "mRS_2",
  age_var = "age_at_stroke",
  nihss_var = "nihss_24_hrs_prior_reperfusion_therapy",
  outcome_label = "for mRS ≤ 2")


```

```{r}
# 4 -- SUBGROUP ANALYSIS OF ORDINAL OUTCOMES --
# Builds upon "evaluate_ordinal_outcome" function defined previously for binary outcome analysis, with wrapper function 


# Wrapper for evaluating subgroups
evaluate_ordinal_subgroups <- function(data, subgroup_labels, subgroup_conditions,
                                       outcome_var, predictor_var,
                                       outcome_label = NULL, n_bootstraps = 100) {
  
  stopifnot(length(subgroup_labels) == length(subgroup_conditions))
  
  for (i in seq_along(subgroup_labels)) {
    label <- subgroup_labels[i]
    condition_expr <- subgroup_conditions[[i]]
    
    subgroup_data <- data %>% dplyr::filter(!!rlang::parse_expr(condition_expr))
    n <- nrow(subgroup_data)
    
    cat("\n----------------------------------------\n")
    cat("Subgroup:", label, "\n")
    cat("N =", n, "\n")
    
    if (n < 30) {
      warning(paste("Subgroup", label, "has < 30 patients. Results may be unstable."))
    }
    
    evaluate_ordinal_outcome(
      data = subgroup_data,
      outcome_var = outcome_var,
      predictor_var = predictor_var,
      title_suffix = paste0("(", label, ") ", outcome_label),
      n_bootstraps = n_bootstraps
    )
  }
}

```

```{r}
# # 4 -- SUBGROUP ANALYSIS OF ORDINAL OUTCOMES --
# EXAMPLE USAGE (stratifying by thrombolysed and non-thrombolysed patients, evaluating for ordinal mRS)

evaluate_ordinal_subgroups(
  data = Hermes_ordinal,
  subgroup_labels = c("No thrombolysis", "Thrombolysis"),
  subgroup_conditions = c('yn_thrombolysed == "No"', 'yn_thrombolysed == "Yes"'),
  outcome_var = "x3_month_mrs",
  predictor_var = "HERMES_Score",
  outcome_label = "for ordinal 3-month mRS",
  n_bootstraps = 100
)


```

```{r}
# 5 -- Temporal model performance --
# Evalutes HERMES-24 performance annually for all years (of sufficient size >30) in the dataset 

compute_annual_auc <- function(data, outcome_var, age_var, nihss_var,
                                negate_score = TRUE, title_suffix = "") {
  
  years <- sort(unique(data$admission_year))
  
  annual_results <- lapply(years, function(yr) {
    year_data <- data[data$admission_year == yr, ]
    
    if (nrow(year_data) < 30) {
      warning(paste("Year", yr, "has < 30 patients — skipping"))
      return(data.frame(year     = yr,
                        auc      = NA,
                        ci_lower = NA,
                        ci_upper = NA,
                        brier    = NA,
                        slope    = NA,
                        intercept = NA,
                        n        = nrow(year_data)))
    }
    
    # Incorporates previous binary outcome analysis function 
    result <- evaluate_binary_outcome(
      data         = year_data,
      outcome_var  = outcome_var,
      age_var      = age_var,
      nihss_var    = nihss_var,
      title_suffix = paste(title_suffix, yr),
      negate_score = negate_score
    )
    
    data.frame(
      year      = yr,
      auc       = result$auc,
      ci_lower  = result$ci_lower,
      ci_upper  = result$ci_upper,
      brier     = result$brier,
      slope     = result$slope,
      intercept = result$intercept,
      n         = result$n
    )
  })
  
  annual_df <- do.call(rbind, annual_results)
  annual_df <- annual_df[!is.na(annual_df$auc), ]
  
  # Plot annual AUC trend
  p <- ggplot(annual_df, aes(x = year, y = auc)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +
    geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),
                  width = 0.2, color = "blue", alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_text(aes(y     = ci_upper,
              label = sprintf("%.2f\n(%.2f–%.2f)", auc, ci_lower, ci_upper)),
          vjust      = -0.3,      # small fixed gap above ci_upper
          hjust      = 0.5,
          size       = 2.5,
          color      = "black",
          lineheight = 0.85) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 2019.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 1.05), breaks = seq(0.5, 1.0, by = 0.1)) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = years) +
    labs(
      title   = paste("Annual AUC trend", title_suffix),
      x       = "Admission year",
      y       = "AUC (95% CI)",
      caption = paste0("n per year: ",
                       paste(paste0(annual_df$year, " (n=", annual_df$n, ")"),
                             collapse = ", "))
    ) +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(
      plot.title   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 7, hjust = 0)
    )
  
  return(list(plot = p, data = annual_df))
}
```

```{r}
# 5 -- Temporal model performance --
# EXAMPLE USAGE - Model performance for binary outcome mRS <=2 for all included years in dataset 

result_mrs2 <- compute_annual_auc(
  data         = Hermes_2_FINAL,
  outcome_var  = "mRS_2",
  age_var      = "age_at_stroke",
  nihss_var    = "nihss_24_hrs_prior_reperfusion_therapy",
  negate_score = TRUE,
  title_suffix = "— mRS ≤2"
)
```

```{r}
# 6 -- Sensitivity to time-point cut-off analysis -- 

sensitivity_cutpoint_analysis <- function(data, outcome_var, age_var, nihss_var,
                                           negate_score = TRUE,
                                           cutpoints = 2018:2021,
                                           n_bootstraps = 2000,
                                           title_suffix = "") {
  
  sensitivity_results <- lapply(cutpoints, function(cut) {
    
    cat(sprintf("\n--- Testing cutpoint: %d ---\n", cut))
    
    pre  <- data[data$admission_year <= cut, ]
    post <- data[data$admission_year >  cut, ]
    
    cat(sprintf("Pre n = %d, Post n = %d\n", nrow(pre), nrow(post)))
    
    # Get AUC for each epoch using your existing function
    result_pre  <- evaluate_binary_outcome(
      data         = pre,
      outcome_var  = outcome_var,
      age_var      = age_var,
      nihss_var    = nihss_var,
      title_suffix = paste("pre", cut),
      negate_score = negate_score
    )
    
    result_post <- evaluate_binary_outcome(
      data         = post,
      outcome_var  = outcome_var,
      age_var      = age_var,
      nihss_var    = nihss_var,
      title_suffix = paste("post", cut),
      negate_score = negate_score
    )
    
       # Bootstrap the DELTA AUC between the two independent cohorts
    set.seed(42)
    
    bootstrap_delta <- function(pre, post, outcome_var, age_var, nihss_var, 
                                negate_score, n_bootstraps) {
      replicate(n_bootstraps, {
        
        s_pre  <- pre[sample(nrow(pre),   replace = TRUE), ]
        s_post <- post[sample(nrow(post), replace = TRUE), ]
        
        # Compute score in each bootstrap sample
        if (negate_score) {
          s_pre$fixed_score  <- -(0.1 * s_pre[[age_var]]  + 1 * s_pre[[nihss_var]])
          s_post$fixed_score <- -(0.1 * s_post[[age_var]] + 1 * s_post[[nihss_var]])
        } else {
          s_pre$fixed_score  <-  (0.1 * s_pre[[age_var]]  + 1 * s_pre[[nihss_var]])
          s_post$fixed_score <-  (0.1 * s_post[[age_var]] + 1 * s_post[[nihss_var]])
        }
        
        # Fit offset model in each bootstrap sample
        model_pre  <- glm(as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ offset(fixed_score)")),
                          data = s_pre,  family = binomial(link = "logit"))
        model_post <- glm(as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ offset(fixed_score)")),
                          data = s_post, family = binomial(link = "logit"))
        
        # Predicted probabilities
        p_pre  <- predict(model_pre,  type = "response")
        p_post <- predict(model_post, type = "response")
        
        # AUC for each bootstrap sample
        auc_pre  <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(pROC::roc(s_pre[[outcome_var]],
                                                     p_pre,
                                                     direction = "<",
                                                     quiet     = TRUE)))
        auc_post <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(pROC::roc(s_post[[outcome_var]],
                                                     p_post,
                                                     direction = "<",
                                                     quiet     = TRUE)))
        auc_post - auc_pre
      })
    }
    
    delta_boot <- bootstrap_delta(
      pre          = pre,
      post         = post,
      outcome_var  = outcome_var,
      age_var      = age_var,
      nihss_var    = nihss_var,
      negate_score = negate_score,
      n_bootstraps = n_bootstraps
    )
    
    # Summarise bootstrap results
    data.frame(
      cutpoint      = cut,
      n_pre         = nrow(pre),
      n_post        = nrow(post),
      auc_pre       = result_pre$auc,
      auc_pre_lo    = result_pre$ci_lower,
      auc_pre_hi    = result_pre$ci_upper,
      auc_post      = result_post$auc,
      auc_post_lo   = result_post$ci_lower,
      auc_post_hi   = result_post$ci_upper,
      delta_auc     = mean(delta_boot),
      delta_ci_lo   = quantile(delta_boot, 0.025),
      delta_ci_hi   = quantile(delta_boot, 0.975),
      p_value       = 2 * min(mean(delta_boot >= 0), mean(delta_boot < 0))
    )
  })
  
  sensitivity_df <- do.call(rbind, sensitivity_results)
  rownames(sensitivity_df) <- NULL
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Plot 1 — AUC by epoch for each cutpoint
  # ----------------------------------------
  
  # Reshape for plotting
  plot_df <- rbind(
    data.frame(cutpoint  = sensitivity_df$cutpoint,
               epoch     = "Pre-cutpoint",
               auc       = sensitivity_df$auc_pre,
               ci_lower  = sensitivity_df$auc_pre_lo,
               ci_upper  = sensitivity_df$auc_pre_hi),
    data.frame(cutpoint  = sensitivity_df$cutpoint,
               epoch     = "Post-cutpoint",
               auc       = sensitivity_df$auc_post,
               ci_lower  = sensitivity_df$auc_post_lo,
               ci_upper  = sensitivity_df$auc_post_hi)
  )
  
  p1 <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = factor(cutpoint), y = auc,
                             color = epoch, group = epoch)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),
                  width = 0.15, alpha = 0.6) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Pre-cutpoint"  = "blue",
                                  "Post-cutpoint" = "red")) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 1.0), breaks = seq(0.5, 1.0, by = 0.1)) +
    labs(
      title  = paste("AUC by epoch across alternative cutpoints", title_suffix),
      x      = "Cutpoint year (pre ≤ year vs post > year)",
      y      = "AUC (95% CI)",
      color  = "Epoch"
    ) +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "bottom")
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Plot 2 — Delta AUC across cutpoints
  # ----------------------------------------
  
    p2 <- ggplot(sensitivity_df, aes(x = factor(cutpoint), y = delta_auc)) +
    geom_point(color = "black", size = 2) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = delta_ci_lo, ymax = delta_ci_hi),
                  width = 0.15, color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    # ΔAUC value and 95% CI label sitting above the upper error bar
    geom_text(aes(y     = delta_ci_hi,
                  label = sprintf("ΔAUC: %.3f\n(%.3f to %.3f)\np=%.3f",
                                  delta_auc,
                                  delta_ci_lo,
                                  delta_ci_hi,
                                  p_value)),
              vjust      = -0.3,
              hjust      = 0.5,
              size       = 2.5,
              color      = "black",
              lineheight = 0.85) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(min(sensitivity_df$delta_ci_lo) - 0.05,
                                  max(sensitivity_df$delta_ci_hi) + 0.15),
                       breaks = seq(-0.3, 0.3, by = 0.05)) +
    labs(
      title    = paste("ΔAUC (post minus pre) across alternative cutpoints", title_suffix),
      subtitle = "Dashed line indicates no difference. Bootstrapped 95% CI shown.",
      x        = "Cutpoint year",
      y        = "ΔAUC (95% CI)"
    ) +
    theme_pubr() +
    theme(plot.title    = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 9))
  
  # ----------------------------------------
  # Combined figure
  # ----------------------------------------
  
  combined <- ggarrange(p1, p2, ncol = 1, nrow = 2, labels = c("A", "B"))
  
  return(list(
    plot      = combined,
    plot_auc  = p1,
    plot_delta = p2,
    data      = sensitivity_df
  ))
}
```

```{r}
# 6 -- Sensitivity to time-point cut-off analysis -- 
# EXAMPLE USAGE - Sensitivity cut-off analysis for 90-day mortality searching for separation in time epoch perfomance with 2018 - 2021 applied as a cut-off period.

sens_mortality <- sensitivity_cutpoint_analysis(
  data         = Hermes_FINAL_vessel,
  outcome_var  = "timedif_mortality_lastknown_90d",
  age_var      = "age_at_stroke",
  nihss_var    = "nihss_24_hrs_prior_reperfusion_therapy",
  negate_score = FALSE,
  cutpoints    = 2018:2021,
  n_bootstraps = 1000,
  title_suffix = "— Mortality"
)
```
